pattern: Only the characters named in the priority string have a required relative order, so counting each character and emitting the prioritized ones in priority order, then the rest, yields a valid arrangement.
hints: Characters absent from the priority string are unconstrained and can go anywhere, so focus on placing only the prioritized ones correctly.
Tally how many of each character the target string holds, then walk the priority string emitting each character that many times.
After emitting all prioritized characters in priority order using the counts, append every remaining character (those not in the priority string) in any consistent order.

edge_inputs.description: one leftover character not in the priority string
priority string longer than needed, extra letters unused
none of s appears in order, all leftovers
every character of s is prioritized

stress.description: random distinct priority string and a random target over lowercase letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = list(string.ascii_lowercase)
    k = rng.randint(1, 26)
    order = rng.sample(letters, k)
    order_s = ''.join(order)
    n = rng.randint(1, min(200, max(1, size)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [order_s, s]
stress.note: order is a sample (unique chars); s freely random
